content: Millet is a grain historically consumed in large amounts by poor, rural populations in Sudan, India, Pakistan, and Africa.
Millet contains goitrogens, compounds that negatively affect the thyroid gland, leading to goiter and thyroid enlargement.
In 1981, investigators found a strong association between high millet consumption and high goiter prevalence in Sudanese schoolchildren.
Traditional processing methods, such as de-hulling and fermentation, can increase the goitrogenic effects of millet.
Semi-wet milling is the only method shown to reduce the goitrogenicity of millet, but it requires specialized equipment.
People consuming gluten-free breads, especially those containing millet, may inadvertently consume excessive millet without realizing it.
